text.title: Shadow Traffic Rollout Pattern Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The first stage is 100% shadow mirror (NOT a 1% real-traffic split). Only after the shadow window passes the gate does any real traffic see the candidate.
rule.rationale: 1% real-traffic split exposes some users to a possibly-worse experience before joint-metric data exists.

rule.statement: Promotion requires ALL THREE within contract for the window: per-request quality delta ≥ -0.02, latency delta ≤ +20%, cost delta ≤ +10%. Any one out of band blocks promotion.
rule.rationale: A model +0.05 quality but +200% cost is not a win. The joint gate forces the trade-off explicit.

rule.statement: Judge model version and prompt are PINNED for the entire shadow window. Calibration audit (≥50 expert-labelled samples) ≤30 days old.
rule.rationale: Judge drift silently invalidates shadow conclusions. Calibration audit aligns judge with expert ground truth.

rule.statement: Minimum: 48 hours AND ≥500 scored requests AND coverage of weekday + weekend patterns. Below any, the gate refuses to evaluate.
rule.rationale: Short windows miss consumer-shaped weekend traffic; n<500 has insufficient power to detect regressions.

rule.statement: After promotion, the previous version becomes the shadow at sampled traffic (≥5%) for ≥7 days as a continuous quality monitor.
rule.rationale: Post-promotion is when novel real-world inputs hit the new model. Continuous shadow is cheap insurance.

rule.statement: Skip when there is no production-mirror infra OR no judge-model calibration exists OR the candidate is a config-only change with no behavior delta.
rule.rationale: Without mirror infra the methodology can't execute; config-only changes don't need shadow.
